ab: nemoso tonsoha no 2022, ’o ’oyonatmopsʉ kaingi ho toʉsvʉsvʉtneni te peela aleonga poa ongko ta cou ’e mivunso.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: os’o tuocosi ’ola auesiesi mi’usnu to ’oyonatmopsʉ ho pnanfi no tata’ia aleonga poa bua coua ’e mivnsoou ta cou ci foinana to you ci pasu’e yasiungu.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年pasu’e yasiungu。
en: I interviewed pasu’e yasiungu, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: mimia yu’tasvʉtʉ hotena seolʉa no tosvo ho uhto emoosi ho aiti.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: to taicosi ne os’o tucocosi, isi eʉsvʉta na isi i’mizi to ongkosi, isi yaeza poa hongneni ’o iachisi emoo ho yousi.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: os’o tuocosi maincis’a na’no bitotonʉ no taezuhi si ongkosu, o’amo ʉmnʉ ’o micu aesiei cila hia tmopsʉa?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: mi’usnu a’o ho yainca, mo’so maica homo boemi to tmapuutu ho tposi ’e ongko, o’aisis’a a’sʉftona etuhca hoisi psoevaha ’e ongkosi, ’o mocmo ci cou lahen’a yainca “巴先生” i’mi tamo maica ho man’i isi koa cocvi.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「巴先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"Ba Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: yainca to pasuya, “na aʉmtʉ ongkoto ’aeno ’e isi uanenicou ho tposi ci ongko na aʉmtʉ ongkoto. ’e ongko’u zou ongko to ’oahngʉ’u, zou ongko to mimia conoemoo, zou ongko mia, tata’ia te asuhcu botngonʉ nate pela e’lʉa na iachi ongko.”
ch: pasuya說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: pasuya said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
